content.text: lets talk about options for the dashboard cache layer before we build anything, what are the tradeoffs

Three options, with tradeoffs: in-memory is fastest but lost on restart; sqlite survives restarts but adds a dependency; a file cache is simplest. On the other hand, the read pattern here is bursty, which favors in-memory. Here is the case for each...
go with sqlite, survives restart matters more than raw speed here
picking the dashboard back up. the cache is evicting too aggressively, look at the ttl logic
The ttl was in seconds but compared against milliseconds. Fixed.
good. add a metric for cache hit rate and expose it on the status page
Added hit-rate metric to the status endpoint.
